title: Non-MLP terminal-op suite
hypothesis: The fused terminal-output path should continue to improve latency and energy on a small non-MLP or otherwise terminal-sensitive model slice once the comparison is staged as corrected-contract reference measurement first and paired fused-vs-non-fused comparison second.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does fused terminal output improve a corrected-contract non-fused nm1 reference across a small non-MLP terminal-sensitive model slice?
include: measurement-only or baseline-refresh non-fused nm1 reference points on a small non-MLP terminal-sensitive suite
paired fused vs non-fused nm1 comparison on the same suite
exclude: broad architecture sweep before the per-model fused comparison is established
follow_on_broad_sweep: run a broader ranking sweep only if the focused non-MLP per-model fused comparisons are positive or mixed enough to justify reintegration

expected_benefit: tests whether the fused win generalizes beyond the original tiny proof
uses the new measurement-only evaluation mode to keep first-stage evidence clean
reduces the risk of reopening broad ranking questions too early
risks: the chosen models may still be too small or too similar to the original proof
model availability may push the work toward suite-building instead of a bounded evaluation
some candidate models may require a quality gate or additional lowering support

expected_result.reason: this first item only records corrected-contract non-fused reference metrics for the selected model slice
the fused path is expected to beat the corrected non-fused references on at least part of the selected terminal-sensitive suite
